title: 10 best herbal tinctures for ovulation pain
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for ovulation pain are liquid extracts from plants used to alleviate discomfort associated with ovulation. These tinctures help by regulating hormonal balances and reducing inflammation. The best herbal tinctures for ovulation pain include Angelica archangelica, Cimicifuga racemosa, Vitex agnus-castus, Paeonia lactiflora, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Lavandula angustifolia, Ginkgo biloba, Trifolium pratense, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, ovulation pain can lead to missed periods, fertility issues, and emotional distress, negatively impacting daily life and relationships.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with ovulation pain because it contains a unique combination of compounds that have been traditionally used to alleviate menstrual cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with ovulation. The bioactive constituents in angelica, such as ligustilide and butylphthalide, work together to relax uterine muscles, reduce inflammation, and stabilize hormone levels. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal angelica tinctures can provide natural relief from ovulation pain, allowing women to experience a more comfortable and manageable menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Angelica's antispasmodic compounds, such as flavonoids and volatile oils, help relax uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms associated with ovulation pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Angelica's anti-inflammatory compounds, including sesquiterpenes and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation in the pelvic region, which may contribute to ovulation discomfort.

Estrogenic and progesteronic properties: Angelica contains phytoestrogens and progestogenic compounds that can help regulate hormonal imbalances, alleviate symptoms of PMS (including ovulation pain), and promote a healthy menstrual cycle.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic Glycosides: These compounds, particularly angelicin, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.

Terpenoids (specifically Sesquiterpenes): Sesquiterpenes present in Angelica archangelica, such as borneol and bornyl acetate, exhibit anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic effects, which may help ease cramping and pain during ovulation.

Alkaloids (specifically Berberine): Berberine, a protoberberine alkaloid found in Angelica archangelica, displays anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can contribute to the reduction of ovulation-related pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica archangelica are commonly used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, including angelic root oil, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation.

Leaves: The leaves of Angelica archangelica are used because they contain flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate ovulation pain.

Stems: The stems of Angelica archangelica are utilized due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to relieve ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and rhizomes in early spring or late summer to ensure optimal potency.
Clean the harvested roots and rhizomes thoroughly with lukewarm water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the cleaned roots and rhizomes into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Steep the chopped roots and rhizomes in a high-proof vodka or ethanol at a 1:5 ratio for 2-6 weeks to extract the active compounds.
Strain the liquid from the jar through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to separate the liquid from the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with ovulation pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the uterus and surrounding tissues. This can reduce cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation, making it an effective natural remedy for managing menstrual cramps. Additionally, hawthorn's ability to dilate blood vessels may also help improve circulation, reducing feelings of heaviness or bloating that often accompany ovulation. By addressing these symptoms, hawthorn tinctures can provide relief and promote a more comfortable ovulation experience.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic and vasodilating effects: Hawthorn's ability to relax blood vessels and improve cardiovascular function can help alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation, by reducing uterine spasms and increasing blood flow.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) have anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the pelvic region, which may contribute to ovulation pain.
Antispasmodic and analgesic effects: Hawthorn's active compounds, such as vitexin and isovitexin, can help relax smooth muscle tissue and alleviate cramps by reducing spasms and pain perception.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help alleviate ovulation pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with menstrual cramps.
Phenolic acids: These compounds found in Crataegus monogyna exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate pain and reduce inflammation during ovulation.

Triterpenoids: These bioactive compounds, such as ursolic acid, demonstrate anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which contribute to the reduction of menstrual pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high content of polyphenols, which are believed to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate ovulation pain.
Buds: The buds are used due to their high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of fresh berries from the shrub early in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the berries at 25 degrees celsius for 72 hours to achieve the optimal level of moisture.
Combine the dried berries with 500ml of 35% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow for the extraction of the active compounds.
Filter the liquid through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with ovulation pain because of its unique ability to ease uterine cramps, which are often intense during this time. The tincture's antispasmodic properties help relax the smooth muscle walls of the uterus, reducing contractions and alleviating discomfort. Additionally, maidenhair tree has natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease pressure on the pelvic area. As a result, many women find relief from the often debilitating cramping associated with ovulation using this natural remedy.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to ovulation pain relief by soothing the uterus and surrounding tissues.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in Ginkgo biloba tincture have a vasodilatory effect, which can help increase blood flow to the pelvic area, reducing cramps and spasms associated with ovulation pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions and contribute to ovulation pain, allowing for a more comfortable menstrual cycle.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: Acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related pain associated with ovulation.
Quercetin: Exhibits potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to ovulation pain.
Ginkgolide B: Inhibits platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound involved in inflammation and pain transmission, which may help alleviate ovulation-related pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids, terpenoids, and bilobalide, which are believed to help alleviate pain and inflammation associated with ovulation.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds contain ginkgotoxin, which has been used to treat various reproductive issues, including ovulation pain due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba contain bioactive compounds that may help reduce pain and inflammation, making them a potential ingredient in ovulation pain relief tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1:2 ratio of ginkgo biloba leaves and branches fresh or dried at peak potency in late summer.
Chop the harvested ginkgo biloba material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped ginkgo biloba with 2 parts 80% grain or vegetable glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid from the jar through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Urtica dioica are commonly used to make tinctures for ovulation pain due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots of Urtica dioica are used to make tinctures for ovulation pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.

Clean the harvested plant material by gently washing it with pure water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 1 part of the cleaned urtica dioica plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with ovulation pain because it has a natural analgesic effect, soothing cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots and stems in early morning when dew is still present on the plant.

Steep 1 part of the chopped plant material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass container for 4-6 weeks.

Grind the dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.

Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking daily, to allow maceration and extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining 30% of the original volume.

The herb's essential oils, particularly camphor and borneol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the uterine lining and reduce spasms in the fallopian tubes. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, help reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping associated with ovulation pain.
Spasmolytic properties: Rosemary's spasmolytic effects, triggered by its essential oils, relax uterine muscles and ease cramps caused by ovulation, providing natural relief from menstrual discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Rosemary tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect the body against oxidative stress and damage caused by ovulatory hormones, reducing inflammation and pain associated with ovulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramps associated with ovulation pain.
Leaves: Fresh rosemary leaves contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate discomfort and relieve bloating during ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosemary leaves at dawn or dusk to ensure maximum potency and efficacy.
Chop 20g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into smaller pieces to facilitate extraction.
Combine the chopped leaves with 200ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring thorough coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction time.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean dark glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with ovulation pain because of its unique properties that aid in reducing inflammation and relaxing uterine cramps. The natural anti-inflammatory compounds present in dandelion root and leaves help to ease discomfort and heaviness in the lower abdomen, while also promoting hormonal balance. Additionally, the bitter compounds in dandelion have a stimulatory effect on the pituitary gland, which can help regulate ovulation and alleviate menstrual cramping associated with ovulation pain. As a result, many women find relief from ovulation-related discomfort when using dandelion tinctures.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion tincture helps to reduce water retention, which is a common symptom of PMS and ovulation pain by increasing urine production and reducing bloating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in dandelion tincture, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpenes, can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pelvic cramps associated with ovulation pain.

Hormone-balancing properties: Dandelion tincture contains phytoestrogens that may help regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to ovulation pain, by modulating estrogen levels and promoting a healthy menstrual cycle.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing pelvic and lower back pain associated with ovulation.
Fruits: The fruits contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve cramping, making them a natural remedy for ovulation discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in mid-morning after dew has dried.
Cleanly chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to release its medicinal properties.
Combine 100g of chopped plant material with 500ml of high-proof vodka or rum in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14-21 days, shaking the jar daily, before straining and filtering the liquid.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles, labeling them with date, batch number, and ingredients used, for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with ovulation pain because it contains methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic that reduces inflammation and relaxes uterine muscles. As the body prepares for ovulation, hormonal fluctuations can cause cramps and discomfort. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties soothe these symptoms, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with ovulation. Additionally, its warming effect helps to increase blood flow, further reducing tension and promoting a sense of relaxation. This natural remedy offers a safe and effective way to manage ovulation pain, allowing women to feel more comfortable during this critical phase of their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Methyl Salicylate: Wintergreen contains methyl salicylate, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin, which helps alleviate cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation.

Gingerol: The presence of gingerol in wintergreen tinctures has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation caused by ovulation, making it an effective remedy for menstrual cramps and bloating.
Menthol: Menthol, a key component of wintergreen, has natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and relieve crampy abdominal pains associated with ovulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps to reduce menstrual cramp discomfort and ease ovulation pain.
Rhyzomes: Contains methyl salicylate, a key compound responsible for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in alleviating ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in late summer or early fall at dawn when dew is present.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and combine it with 100 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:3 for one hour.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid.
Label and store the final tincture at room temperature, ready to use as a 10-20 drop dose three times a day for ovulation pain relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with ovulation pain because they contain phytoestrogens that mimic the body's natural estrogen levels, promoting a balanced hormonal environment. As ovulation approaches, chaste tree's bioactive compounds stimulate the pituitary gland to release luteinizing hormone, which triggers ovulation and reduces cramping and discomfort associated with this process. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate uterine contractions, further minimizing pain during ovulation. By supporting hormonal regulation and relaxation of the uterine muscles, chaste tree tinctures can significantly reduce ovulation-related pain.
remedy_properties: Phytoestrogens: Chaste tree contains phytoestrogens, which can help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce symptoms associated with PMS, such as cramping and breast tenderness, by binding to estrogen receptors in the body.

Terpene compounds: Terpene compounds present in chaste tree tinctures have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort during ovulation and menstruation by reducing inflammation and relaxing uterine muscles.

Vitex agnus-castus extract: The extract of Chaste Tree (Vitex agnus-castus) has been shown to have a regulatory effect on the pituitary gland, helping to normalize hormonal imbalances and stimulate ovulation, which can reduce ovulation pain and related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in relieving cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Seeds: The seeds contain a high concentration of flavonoids and terpenes, which help to relax the uterine muscles and reduce menstrual cramp severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vitex agnus castus leaves and flowers for a total of 2 ounces.
Combine the herb material with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1 part herb to 4 parts vodka.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 6-8 weeks shaking occasionally.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the clear liquid to dark glass bottles and label with date prepared and contents using a permanent marker.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ovulation pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are dysmenorrhea, PMS symptoms, and endometriosis. Certain herbs like cramp bark, black haw, and blue cohosh have been traditionally used to alleviate menstrual cramps, mood swings, and breast tenderness associated with these conditions. Herbal tinctures can be made from these herbs and taken as a supplement or added to teas for relief.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with ovulation pain is a blend of Maca root, Red Clover, and Wild Yam. Maca root helps regulate hormonal balance and alleviate menstrual cramps. Red Clover supports uterine health and eases ovulation-related pain. Wild Yam, rich in diosgenin, has natural progesterone-like properties that soothe the uterine lining and ease cramping. Combining these three tinctures can provide relief from ovulation pain and promote overall reproductive health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Angelica archangelica tinctures helps with ovulation pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The roots and rhizomes of the Angelica plant contain compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which have been shown to relax the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms associated with ovulation. Additionally, Angelica's estrogenic properties may help regulate hormone imbalances, potentially alleviating pain caused by ovulatory issues. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to soothe menstrual discomfort, making it a popular choice for women experiencing ovulation pain.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic glycosides: These compounds help with ovulation pain by reducing inflammation and modulating hormone levels, alleviating menstrual cramps and discomfort.

Alkaloids (specifically, berberine): Berberine in Angelica archangelica helps with ovulation pain by having anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which reduce muscle cramps and spasms associated with menstrual pain.

Essential oils (specifically, sesquiterpene lactones and monoterpenes): These compounds help with ovulation pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which reduce pain and discomfort associated with menstrual cycles.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties to relieve cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Leaves: Employed for their warming and antispasmodic properties to help ease menstrual cramps and other symptoms of ovulation pain.
Buds: Utilized for their expectorant and antispasmodic properties to help alleviate respiratory and abdominal discomforts associated with ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and leaves in late summer or early fall when they are fully mature.
Clean and dry the harvested roots and leaves for at least 2 weeks or until completely dry.
Chop the dried roots and leaves into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped roots and leaves with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1 part plant material to 2 parts vodka.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking it every day to facilitate extraction.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cimicifuga racemosa tinctures helps with ovulation pain because it is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to alleviate menstrual discomfort. (Note that the list only includes 2 elements, but I will include a third element that is sometimes used) Leaves: Used in some preparations due to their content of volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, which may contribute to their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried cimicifuga racemosa roots by digging up the roots in the fall season.
Clean the roots thoroughly with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces using a sharp knife and weigh 1 part to 2 parts of vodka.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar and steep for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus-castus tinctures helps with ovulation pain because it is believed to regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to cramps and discomfort during ovulation. remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of chaste berries and flavonoids, which are believed to have a regulating effect on the menstrual cycle and alleviate associated pain.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part dried vitex agnus-castus berries and 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Combine the vitex agnus-castus berries with 2 cups 80 proof vodka in the glass container.
Seal the container and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Paeonia lactiflora tinctures helps with ovulation pain because it is believed to have a profound effect on the body's natural pain relief pathways. Saponins: Saponins in Paeonia lactiflora tinctures help soothe and calm the uterine muscles, thereby alleviating cramps and discomfort during ovulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of paeonol, an active compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps.

Barks: They are used due to their content of paeonol and other active compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to soothe menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh paeonia lactiflora roots and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are mature.
Chop the harvested roots and flowers into small pieces and dry them in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried roots and flowers in a clean glass jar and add 50% vodka and 50% water to create a tincture.
Seal the jar and allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 6 to 8 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth and bottle it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with ovulation pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax the uterine muscles. The active compounds, gingerols and shogaols, have been shown to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause cramping and discomfort during ovulation. By reducing inflammation and relaxing the uterine muscles, Zingiber officinale tinctures provide relief from ovulation pain, allowing women to feel more comfortable and at ease during this time. Shogaols: Shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce the severity of ovulation pain and promote a sense of comfort.

Gingerol-derived sesquiterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate ovulation pain by reducing inflammation and blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots are also a key component in ginger tinctures, as they contain similar active compounds to rhizomes, making them effective in reducing pain and inflammation.
Stems: Stems of the ginger plant, although less commonly used, still contain some active compounds, which can contribute to the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects of the tincture.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning after the dew has dried.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and dry them in a warm place for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried roots with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it daily for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids, transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with ovulation pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tannins and flavonoids present in the root of the licorice plant have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping associated with ovulation. The tincture's ability to relax the uterine muscles and regulate hormonal imbalances can also contribute to its pain-relieving effects, making it a potentially useful natural remedy for women experiencing discomfort during ovulation. remedy_constituents: Saponins: Help alleviate ovulation pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's immune response, which may contribute to cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Flavonoids: Act as potent antioxidants, which can help minimize oxidative stress and inflammation that may exacerbate ovulation pain, promoting a more comfortable and balanced menstrual cycle.

Glycyrrhizin: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help relieve muscle cramps and spasms associated with ovulation, making it a potentially effective constituent in managing ovulation pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part of Glycyrrhiza glabra due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures due to their ability to balance the body's hormonal levels, which can help alleviate ovulation pain.
Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help ease cramps and discomfort associated with ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of dried root of glycyrrhiza glabra in the early morning when its moisture content is lowest.
Chop the dried root into small pieces and mix it with 500 grams of vodka at 35% abv.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a lid and let it macerate in a cool dark place for 14 days.
Shake the jar daily to facilitate the extraction of the active compounds from the root into the vodka.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then bottle the tincture in dark glass bottles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia tinctures helps with ovulation pain because of its ability to promote relaxation and reduce inflammation. The tincture's calming properties can help alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation. The lavender plant's active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that can soothe the uterine muscles and ease pain. remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic, providing relief from pain and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits sedative properties, helping to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate ovulation pain, promoting relaxation and calmness.
Lavandulol: Demonstrates spasmolytic activity, easing muscle contractions in the reproductive organs that can cause ovulation pain, promoting a sense of comfort and relief.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their calming and analgesic properties.
Flowers: They are used because they contain bioactive compounds that help reduce pain and promote relaxation.
Stems: They are used for their potential anti-inflammatory effects, which may help alleviate ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Dry the flowers in a warm place or using a food dehydrator for 1-2 weeks to prevent mold.
Combine 1 part dried flowers with 2 parts 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar for tincture preparation.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with ovulation pain because of its unique ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation. This herb contains flavonoids and terpenoids that act as natural vasodilators, helping to relax blood vessels and reduce cramping. By enhancing oxygenation and nutrient delivery to the reproductive organs, Ginkgo biloba tinctures can alleviate ovulation pain and discomfort. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to soothe and calm the body, providing relief from the intense pain associated with ovulation.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with ovulation pain by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the reproductive organs.
Bilobalide: This terpene helps alleviate ovulation pain by modulating the activity of neurotransmitters and hormones, reducing the severity of cramps.
Quercetin: This phenolic compound helps with ovulation pain by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent and antioxidant, which helps to reduce the oxidative stress associated with menstrual cramps.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba plant are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate ovulation pain.
Buds: The buds of the Ginkgo biloba plant are also used to make tinctures due to their rich content of bioactive compounds that may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with ovulation.

Seeds: The seeds of the Ginkgo biloba plant contain a compound called ginkgolic acid, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making them a popular choice for tinctures aimed at alleviating ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 part of the stem in the morning when the dew is still present.
Dry the harvested ginkgo biloba leaves and stems in a well-ventilated area at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 1 part of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and stems with 2 parts of 70% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional agitation to ensure proper extraction.
Strain and filter the liquid through a coffee filter to remove the ginkgo biloba solids and obtain the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Trifolium pratense tinctures helps with ovulation pain because they contain isoflavones and other bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to soothe and calm the reproductive system, reducing discomfort and pain associated with ovulation. Additionally, Trifolium pratense is believed to regulate hormonal balance, promoting a healthy menstrual cycle and alleviating symptoms of ovulation pain. This natural remedy provides a holistic approach to managing ovulation pain, promoting overall reproductive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and estrogenic properties, which can help alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with ovulation.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid with analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, quercetin can help reduce pain and inflammation during ovulation.
Formononetin: This prenylated isoflavone has shown anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate symptoms of ovulation pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain isoflavones, which help in reducing menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with ovulation pain.
Leaves: Leaves are also rich in isoflavones and other compounds that contribute to their pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Red Clover contain a higher concentration of isoflavones than the leaves or flowers, making them a preferred choice for tinctures to alleviate ovulation pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried trifolium pratense flowers and 50g of 60% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Combine the dried trifolium pratense flowers and 50g of 60% ethanol in the glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container discarding the solids.
Store the trifolium pratense tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tinctures helps with ovulation pain because it has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that ease menstrual cramps associated with ovulation. The curcumin present in Curcuma longa has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions, leading to pain. Additionally, curcumin has natural analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties that reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, providing relief from ovulation pain and discomfort. Regular use of Curcuma longa tinctures may help alleviate this common issue.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Helps with ovulation pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to menstrual cramps and discomfort.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): Contributes to the reduction of ovulation pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to menstrual cramps.
Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): Helps alleviate ovulation pain by modulating the body's pain response and reducing the release of pain-causing chemicals, such as prostaglandins and bradykinin.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Used due to their high concentration of curcuminoids, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties to alleviate ovulation pain.
Roots: Employed for their bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and modulate hormone levels, contributing to pain relief associated with ovulation.
Leaves: Utilized for their essential oils and bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, providing comfort during ovulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh curcuma longa roots in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the roots into small pieces and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Combine the dried roots with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by weight and transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place and let it steep for 2 to 3 weeks shaking it every day to aid infusion.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture.
